decision_summary: The chip temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). Previous attempts to increase spreader conductivity hit the upper bound (500 W/mK), and geometry changes are constrained by legality limits. The most effective remaining lever is to increase the base plate conductivity to improve heat extraction from the spreader to the side boundaries. We will increase base_material.conductivity from 12.0 to 24.0 (2x step, within bounds [5.0, 250.0]).

changes.reason: Increasing base plate conductivity enhances lateral heat spreading in the lower domain and improves conduction path to the Dirichlet boundaries at x=0 and x=1.2, helping to reduce the chip peak temperature.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature towards the 85.0°C constraint.
More uniform temperature distribution in the base plate.
Improved overall thermal resistance of the assembly.
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts entirely to the interface between chip and spreader.
Large jumps in conductivity can sometimes cause solver convergence issues, though LU solver is robust for this scale.
